title: Microsoft Defender for Endpoint - Advanced Hunting API (KQL)
applies_to: Running KQL queries against Microsoft Defender TVM and device telemetry from Logic Apps or other automation
Exporting Defender vulnerability, device, and software data via the Advanced Hunting endpoint
prerequisites: Azure app registration with client credentials (client_id + client_secret)
OAuth2 token obtained for audience https://api.securitycenter.microsoft.com

confirmed_facts.fact: The Advanced Hunting endpoint is POST https://api.securitycenter.microsoft.com/api/advancedqueries/run and accepts a JSON body with a single 'Query' string field containing KQL.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — confirmed working in production

confirmed_facts.fact: The OAuth2 token scope for this endpoint is https://api.securitycenter.microsoft.com/.default (legacy securitycenter audience, not api.security.microsoft.com).
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — using this scope with client credentials produces a valid Bearer token accepted by the endpoint

confirmed_facts.fact: The response schema is an object with three top-level fields: Stats (execution metadata), Schema (array of {Name, Type} column descriptors), and Results (array of data row objects).
Downstream actions should always access body('action_name')?['Results'] to get the data rows. Stats and Schema are diagnostic only.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — Create CSV Table action uses @body('Parse_JSON_-_Devices')?['Results']

confirmed_facts.fact: Large KQL result sets can hit Logic Apps response size limits. Use '| take 50000' in the KQL query to cap output when the dataset may be very large.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — Desktops_Legacy branch uses '| take 50000' for CVEs older than 365 days

confirmed_facts.fact: The HTTP action for this endpoint requires runtimeConfiguration.contentTransfer.transferMode: 'Chunked' to handle large result sets without truncation.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — all HTTP calls to this endpoint set transferMode Chunked

confirmed_facts.fact: DeviceTvmSoftwareVulnerabilities and DeviceTvmSoftwareVulnerabilitiesKB must be joined on CveId. CveId from the KB table must be cast with extend CveId = tostring(CveId) before the join.

KQL OS segmentation patterns that work in production: non-Windows devices use 'where OSPlatform !startswith "Windows"'; Windows Server uses 'where OSPlatform startswith "WindowsServer"'; Windows 10/11 desktops use 'where OSPlatform startswith "Windows1"'.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — four parallel queries targeting different OS slices

confirmed_facts.fact: Date-based filtering uses 'where PublishedDate > ago(365d)' for recent vulnerabilities and 'where PublishedDate < ago(365d)' for legacy/historical ones.
confirmed_facts.source: production Logic App — Desktops vs Desktops_Legacy branch split

kql_patterns.join_tvm_kb:
DeviceTvmSoftwareVulnerabilities
| join kind=inner (
    DeviceTvmSoftwareVulnerabilitiesKB
    | extend CveId = tostring(CveId)
    | project CveId, VulnerabilitySeverityLevel, CvssScore, PublishedDate, VulnerabilityDescription
) on CveId
| project OSPlatform, CVE = CveId, CvssScore, Risk = VulnerabilitySeverityLevel,
    Host = DeviceName, OSVersion, SoftwareName, VulnerabilityDescription,
    Solution = RecommendedSecurityUpdate, SoftwareVendor, SoftwareVersion, PublishedDate

items.description: One object per query column. Properties match the projected KQL field names.

implementation_notes: Always use the securitycenter audience (https://api.securitycenter.microsoft.com/.default) for this endpoint, not the newer api.security.microsoft.com audience. Mixing them causes 403 errors.

Always add a ParseJson action after the HTTP call. Access Results through the null-safe operator: body('Parse_JSON_-_Devices')?['Results']. The ?['field'] form is required because Stats or Results may be absent on error responses.

For parallel data pulls (e.g., Devices, Servers, Desktops), all HTTP actions can share the same runAfter dependency on the token ParseJson step. No parallel container is needed — they run concurrently automatically.
Split large OS segments into separate queries rather than one combined query. The 50,000 row limit applies per query, and different OS slices have different data volumes and freshness profiles.
When the dataset is time-segmented (e.g., recent vs. legacy), use date filters in KQL rather than post-filtering in Logic Apps. This keeps response size predictable.
The Table action (type: Table, format: CSV) applied to a Results array produces a flat CSV from the projected KQL columns. Column order matches the project statement in the KQL.
Do not include Stats or Schema in the CSV output. Only pass Results to the Table action.
common_failures: Using https://api.security.microsoft.com/.default as the token scope — this audience is rejected by the securitycenter endpoint.
Omitting contentTransfer.transferMode: Chunked on the HTTP action — large result sets are truncated silently.
Forgetting extend CveId = tostring(CveId) in the KB table before the join — the join fails or produces no rows.
Accessing body('action')?['Results'] before the ParseJson step — the raw HTTP body is not typed and the expression fails at runtime.
Running one combined query for all OS types — a single query across all devices can hit the 50,000 row limit and lose data from some segments.
